description.summary: Psychic phenomena encompass a range of purported mental abilities that operate beyond normal sensory channels and known physical laws.
description.full: Psychic phenomena encompass a range of purported mental abilities that operate beyond normal sensory channels and known physical laws. These include extrasensory perception (ESP) such as telepathy, clairvoyance, and precognition, as well as psychokinesis (mind-over-matter effects). While reported across all cultures and time periods, psychic phenomena remain controversial. Parapsychology emerged as a scientific discipline to study these effects, with notable research programs at Duke University, Stanford Research Institute, and various government agencies including the CIA's Stargate Project. Despite thousands of experiments spanning nearly a century, debates continue regarding replicability, methodology, and theoretical frameworks. The field encompasses famous cases like Uri Geller's alleged metal-bending, Edgar Cayce's trance readings, and Ingo Swann's remote viewing protocols, alongside rigorous laboratory research and ongoing consciousness studies.
